reasoning: The "Coalition of the Willing" (COW) plans for deploying forces to Ukraine have definitively stalled and shifted into a purely future-tense, post-ceasefire contingency. Recent official declarations (as of August 2025) confirm that the coalition is only ready to send troops *after* a cessation of hostilities. With no ceasefire currently in place or imminent, deploying a massive multinational peacekeeping/reassurance force (even a preliminary HQ or training element) before the end of December 2025 is logistically and politically impossible. Mentions of 'interceptors' at recent summits are specifically regarding the donation/sale and financing of air defense systems (like Patriots) for Ukrainian forces to operate, which explicitly does not trigger resolution. Because a ceasefire agreement and a complex multinational deployment would take far longer than the remaining 4.5 months of the year, the likelihood of this resolving YES is effectively zero. I am forecasting the minimum allowable probability of 0.02.
